query_normalized: predicting amine reactivity using graph neural networks and public databases chemistry
rationale: The paper focuses on document classification using natural language processing techniques, which is an off-domain application of neural networks compared to the user's question about predicting chemical amine reactivity using Graph Neural Networks on molecular data. This falls under the rejection rule for "Distinct construct sharing only homonym keywords" (neural networks) and "Off-domain entirely" (text processing vs. computational chemistry).
